description: Implement the complete Liu Yao foundation data layer and hexagram arrangement (Pai Gua) engine. This includes all base data tables (Ba Gua, 64 hexagrams, Na Jia, Liu Qin, Wu Xing, Tian Gan Di Zhi, Shi Ying positions, Liu Shen, Xun Kong), the Gan-Zhi calendar conversion using sxtwl, and the hexagram arrangement logic that takes coin tosses + date and produces a fully annotated hexagram with all attributes.
steps: Create the package structure: /projects/sandbox/zhushi.liuyao/liuyao/__init__.py, liuyao/data.py (all static data), liuyao/calendar_utils.py (Gan-Zhi conversion), liuyao/hexagram.py (hexagram arrangement), liuyao/main.py (CLI entry point), tests/__init__.py, tests/test_hexagram.py

In liuyao/data.py implement: (a) TIAN_GAN (10 Heavenly Stems with Wu Xing attributes), (b) DI_ZHI (12 Earthly Branches with Wu Xing attributes), (c) WU_XING cycle (sheng/ke relationships), (d) BA_GUA (8 trigrams with binary representation, name, Wu Xing attribute), (e) 64_HEXAGRAMS lookup (upper+lower trigram to hexagram name and palace), (f) NA_JIA table (the Heavenly Stem and Earthly Branch assigned to each line of each trigram per the Na Jia system), (g) LIU_QIN (Six Relations: derive from self-palace Wu Xing vs line Wu Xing), (h) SHI_YING positions (the world/response line positions for each of the 64 hexagrams based on palace change rules), (i) LIU_SHEN (Six Spirits assignment based on day stem), (j) XUN_KONG (void calculation based on day pillar), (k) SAN_HE (Three Harmony combinations: Si-You-Chou=Metal, Shen-Zi-Chen=Water, Hai-Mao-Wei=Wood, Yin-Wu-Xu=Fire), (l) LIU_CHONG (Six Clashes), (m) LIU_HE (Six Harmonies), (n) CHANG_SHENG_12 (Twelve Life Stages for each element), (o) HUA_JIN_SHEN/HUA_TUI_SHEN (advance/retreat patterns: Yin->Mao, Shen->You, Chou->Chen, Wei->Xu for advance; reverse for retreat)

In liuyao/calendar_utils.py: Use sxtwl library to convert a Gregorian date (year, month, day) to: (a) the Gan-Zhi year pillar, (b) the Gan-Zhi month pillar (based on Jie Qi, the solar terms - Yin month starts at Li Chun ~Feb 4), (c) the Gan-Zhi day pillar. Also provide a function to get the month branch (Yue Jian) and day branch (Ri Chen) which are used for wang-shuai analysis. Handle the special case of 23:00-00:00 belonging to next day's Zi hour.

In liuyao/hexagram.py: Implement class Hexagram that: (a) Takes 6 coin-toss results (each being 6/7/8/9 representing old-yin/young-yang/young-yin/old-yang) and a date, (b) Determines the original hexagram (Ben Gua) and changed hexagram (Bian Gua), (c) Identifies which lines are moving (dong yao), (d) Looks up the palace (Gong) the hexagram belongs to, (e) Assigns Na Jia (stem+branch) to each line, (f) Assigns Liu Qin (six relations) based on palace Wu Xing vs line Wu Xing, (g) Determines Shi (world) and Ying (response) positions, (h) Assigns Liu Shen based on day stem, (i) Calculates Xun Kong (void) based on day pillar, (j) Does the same for Bian Gua lines. Provide a display method that prints the hexagram in traditional format.

In liuyao/main.py: Create a CLI that accepts: (a) date in YYYY-MM-DD format, (b) 6 coin-toss values (space-separated, each 6/7/8/9), OR (c) hexagram name directly. Output the fully arranged hexagram. Also support an alternative input mode where user provides hexagram name + moving lines.

In tests/test_hexagram.py: Write tests verifying: (a) Na Jia assignment is correct for known hexagrams (e.g., Qian Gua should have Jia-Zi, Jia-Yin, Jia-Chen for inner trigram), (b) Palace lookup is correct, (c) Shi-Ying positions match known values, (d) Calendar conversion gives correct Gan-Zhi for known dates, (e) A complete coin-toss input produces the expected hexagram arrangement matching the format shown in the theory documents.
acceptance_criteria: Running `python3 -c "from liuyao.hexagram import Hexagram; h = Hexagram([8,7,7,9,7,8], 2024, 1, 15); h.display()"` produces a correctly formatted hexagram with Na Jia, Liu Qin, Shi/Ying, and Liu Shen annotations
Running `python3 -m pytest tests/test_hexagram.py -v` passes all tests
Calendar conversion correctly identifies month branch based on solar terms (e.g., Jan 15 2024 is still Chou month before Li Chun)
Moving lines (lao-yang=9, lao-yin=6) correctly generate the Bian Gua (changed hexagram)
verification: Run `python3 -m pytest tests/test_hexagram.py -v` and confirm all tests pass
Run `python3 -c "from liuyao.hexagram import Hexagram; h = Hexagram([8,7,7,9,7,8], 2024, 1, 15); h.display()"` and verify output format
findings: All 44 tests pass. pytest needed to be installed (pip3 install pytest). Fixed trigram binary encoding (initial values had 兑/巽/震/艮 swapped). The sxtwl library uses 0-indexed values for tg/dz. Calendar correctly handles Li Chun boundary for year pillar and Jie Qi for month pillar. The 64-hexagram palace generation algorithm works correctly with the palace derivation rules.
